array.description: An ordered map in PHP. Arrays can hold any combination of values indexed by integers or strings. They serve as lists, hash tables, dictionaries, collections, stacks, and queues.

array_push.description: Pushes one or more elements onto the end of an array. Returns the new number of elements.

array_pop.description: Pops and returns the last element of the array, shortening it by one.

array_shift.description: Shifts the first value off the array and returns it, re-indexing numeric keys.

array_unshift.description: Prepends one or more elements to the beginning of an array.

array_merge.description: Merges one or more arrays. Numeric keys are re-indexed; string keys are overwritten by later arrays.

array_combine.description: Creates an array by using one array for keys and another for its values.

array_map.description: Applies the callback to each element and returns a new array of results.

array_filter.description: Filters elements using a callback. Without callback, removes falsy values.

array_reduce.description: Iteratively reduces the array to a single value using a callback.

array_keys.signature: array_keys(array $array, mixed $filter_value = null, bool $strict = false): array
array_keys.description: Returns all the keys or a subset of the keys of an array.

array_values.description: Returns all the values from the array with numeric indexing.

array_unique.description: Removes duplicate values from an array, preserving the first occurrence.

array_reverse.description: Returns an array with elements in reverse order.

array_slice.signature: array_slice(array $array, int $offset, ?int $length = null, bool $preserve_keys = false): array
array_slice.description: Extracts a slice of the array.

array_splice.description: Removes a portion of the array and optionally replaces it. Returns removed elements.

array_search.description: Searches the array for a value and returns the corresponding key.

in_array.description: Checks if a value exists in an array.

count.description: Counts all elements in an array or Countable object.

sort.description: Sorts an array in ascending order by value, re-indexing keys.

usort.description: Sorts an array by values using a user-defined comparison function.

array_walk.description: Applies a user function to every member of an array.

array_key_exists.description: Checks if the given key or index exists in the array.

array_column.description: Returns the values from a single column of a multi-dimensional array.

array_chunk.description: Splits an array into chunks of the specified size.

array_diff.description: Computes the difference of arrays using values for comparison.

array_intersect.description: Computes the intersection of arrays using values for comparison.

array_fill.description: Fills an array with values, specifying start index and number of entries.

compact.description: Creates an array containing variables and their values.

range.description: Creates an array containing a range of elements.

string.description: A series of bytes in PHP. Strings are binary-safe and can be up to 2GB. String literals can be specified in four ways: single-quoted, double-quoted, heredoc, and nowdoc.

strlen.description: Returns the length of the given string in bytes.

strpos.description: Finds the position of the first occurrence of a substring.

substr.description: Returns the portion of string specified by offset and length.

str_replace.signature: str_replace(array|string $search, array|string $replace, string|array $subject, int &$count = null): string|array
str_replace.description: Replaces all occurrences of the search string with the replacement.

str_contains.description: (PHP 8.0+) Determines if a string contains a given substring.

str_starts_with.description: (PHP 8.0+) Checks if a string starts with a given substring.

str_ends_with.description: (PHP 8.0+) Checks if a string ends with a given substring.

strtolower.description: Returns a string with all ASCII alphabetic characters converted to lowercase.

strtoupper.description: Returns a string with all ASCII alphabetic characters converted to uppercase.

trim.description: Strips whitespace or other characters from both ends of a string.

explode.description: Splits a string by a string delimiter and returns an array.

implode.description: Joins array elements with a glue string.

str_pad.signature: str_pad(string $string, int $length, string $pad_string = ' ', int $pad_type = STR_PAD_RIGHT): string
str_pad.description: Pads a string to a certain length with another string.

str_repeat.description: Repeats a string a given number of times.

sprintf.description: Returns a string produced according to a format string.

htmlspecialchars.signature: htmlspecialchars(string $string, int $flags = ENT_QUOTES | ENT_SUBSTITUTE, ?string $encoding = null, bool $double_encode = true): string
htmlspecialchars.description: Converts special characters to HTML entities for safe output.

urlencode.description: URL-encodes a string, replacing special characters with percent-encoded values.

base64_encode.description: Encodes the given data with Base64.

DateTime.description: Representation of date and time. Allows manipulation of dates and times with timezone support. For immutable operations, use DateTimeImmutable instead.

format.description: Returns date formatted according to given format. Common: Y-m-d, H:i:s, U (timestamp), c (ISO 8601).

modify.description: Alters the timestamp by incrementing or decrementing in a format accepted by strtotime(). E.g. '+1 day', '-2 months'.

diff.description: Returns the difference between two DateTime objects as a DateInterval.

getTimestamp.description: Gets the Unix timestamp for the DateTime object.

createFromFormat.description: Parses a date string according to a specified format. Returns false on failure.

setTimezone.description: Sets the time zone for the DateTime object.

add.description: Modifies the DateTime object by adding the given DateInterval.

sub.description: Modifies the DateTime object by subtracting the given DateInterval.

DateTimeImmutable.description: Immutable representation of date and time. Unlike DateTime, modification methods return a new instance instead of modifying the existing one.

format.description: Returns date formatted according to given format.

modify.description: Creates a new object with modified timestamp. The original is not changed.

diff.description: Returns the difference between two DateTimeInterface objects.

createFromFormat.description: Parses a date string according to a specified format.

setTimezone.description: Returns a new instance with the specified time zone.

add.description: Returns a new object with the given DateInterval added.

sub.description: Returns a new object with the given DateInterval subtracted.

SplStack.description: Provides the main functionalities of a stack implemented using a doubly linked list. Iteration is LIFO (Last In, First Out).

push.description: Pushes an element at the top of the stack.

pop.description: Pops a node from the top of the stack.

top.description: Peeks at the top of the stack without removing it.

isEmpty.description: Checks whether the stack is empty.

count.description: Returns the number of elements in the stack.

SplQueue.description: Provides the main functionalities of a queue implemented using a doubly linked list. Iteration is FIFO (First In, First Out).

enqueue.description: Adds an element to the end of the queue.

dequeue.description: Dequeues a node from the front of the queue.

bottom.description: Peeks at the node from the beginning of the queue.

isEmpty.description: Checks whether the queue is empty.

count.description: Returns the number of elements in the queue.

ArrayObject.signature: class ArrayObject implements IteratorAggregate, ArrayAccess, Serializable, Countable
ArrayObject.description: Allows objects to work as arrays. Provides array-like access with bracket syntax and iteration, with the flexibility of an object.

offsetGet.description: Returns the value at the specified index.

offsetSet.description: Sets the value at the specified index.

offsetExists.description: Returns whether the requested index exists.

offsetUnset.description: Unsets the value at the specified index.

append.description: Appends the value as the last element.

count.description: Returns the number of public properties or array elements.

getArrayCopy.description: Creates a copy of the ArrayObject as a standard array.

getIterator.description: Creates an external iterator for iterating over the object.

strlen.description: Returns the length of the given string in bytes. For multi-byte character length, use mb_strlen() instead.

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The string being measured for length

strpos.description: Finds the numeric position of the first occurrence of needle in the haystack string.

substr.description: Returns the portion of string specified by the offset and length parameters.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Start position; negative counts from end

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Maximum number of characters to return

str_replace.signature: str_replace(array|string $search, array|string $replace, string|array $subject, int &$count = null): string|array
str_replace.description: Replaces all occurrences of the search string with the replacement string. Works with both strings and arrays.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The string or array being searched and replaced on

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: If passed, set to the number of replacements performed

str_contains.description: (PHP 8.0+) Performs a case-sensitive check indicating if needle is contained in haystack.

str_starts_with.description: (PHP 8.0+) Checks if a string starts with a given substring. Case-sensitive.

parameters.name: needle
parameters.description: The substring to search for at the start

str_ends_with.description: (PHP 8.0+) Checks if a string ends with a given substring. Case-sensitive.

parameters.name: needle
parameters.description: The substring to search for at the end

strtolower.description: Returns a string with all ASCII alphabetic characters converted to lowercase.

strtoupper.description: Returns a string with all ASCII alphabetic characters converted to uppercase.

trim.description: Strips whitespace (or other characters) from the beginning and end of a string.

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The string that will be trimmed

explode.description: Returns an array of strings, each of which is a substring of string formed by splitting on the separator.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of elements in the result

implode.description: Joins array elements with a glue string. join() is an alias.

parameters.name: separator
parameters.description: The glue string placed between elements

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: The array of strings to join

str_pad.signature: str_pad(string $string, int $length, string $pad_string = ' ', int $pad_type = STR_PAD_RIGHT): string
str_pad.description: Pads a string to a certain length with another string.

parameters.name: length
parameters.description: Desired length of the final string

str_repeat.description: Returns a string repeated the specified number of times.

sprintf.description: Returns a string produced according to the formatting string format.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format string with conversion specifiers

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Values to insert into format specifiers

htmlspecialchars.signature: htmlspecialchars(string $string, int $flags = ENT_QUOTES | ENT_SUBSTITUTE, ?string $encoding = null, bool $double_encode = true): string
htmlspecialchars.description: Converts special characters to HTML entities. Essential for XSS prevention.

parameters.name: double_encode
parameters.description: Whether to encode existing HTML entities

urlencode.description: URL-encodes a string, replacing special characters with percent-encoded values. Spaces become +.

base64_encode.description: Encodes data with MIME base64. Designed to make binary data survive transport through non-binary-safe channels.

array_push.description: Pushes one or more elements onto the end of an array. Equivalent to $array[] = $value for a single value.

array_pop.description: Pops and returns the last element of the array, shortening the array by one element.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: The array to get the value from

array_shift.description: Shifts the first value off the array and returns it. Numeric keys are re-indexed starting from zero.

array_unshift.description: Prepends one or more elements to the beginning of an array. All numerical keys are re-indexed.

array_merge.description: Merges the elements of one or more arrays. Numeric keys are re-indexed; string keys are overwritten by later arrays.

array_map.description: Applies the callback to the elements of the given arrays and returns a new array containing the results.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Callback function to apply to each element

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: Array to run through the callback

array_filter.description: Filters elements of an array using a callback function. Keys are preserved.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: The callback function; if null, removes falsy values

array_reduce.description: Iteratively reduces the array to a single value using a callback function.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Function receiving (carry, item) and returning carry

array_keys.signature: array_keys(array $array, mixed $filter_value = null, bool $strict = false): array
array_keys.description: Returns all the keys or a subset of the keys of an array.

parameters.name: filter_value
parameters.description: If specified, only keys containing this value are returned

array_values.description: Returns all the values from the array and indexes the array numerically.

array_unique.description: Removes duplicate values from an array. The first occurrence of each value is kept.

array_reverse.description: Returns an array with elements in reverse order.

array_slice.signature: array_slice(array $array, int $offset, ?int $length = null, bool $preserve_keys = false): array
array_slice.description: Extracts a slice of the array.

array_splice.description: Removes a portion of the array and replaces it with something else. Returns the removed elements.

parameters.name: replacement
parameters.description: Elements to insert at the offset

array_search.description: Searches the array for a given value and returns the first corresponding key if successful.

in_array.description: Checks if a value exists in an array.

count.description: Counts all elements in an array or a Countable object.

sort.description: Sorts an array in ascending order by value. Existing keys are discarded and the array is re-indexed.

usort.description: Sorts an array by values using a user-defined comparison function.

array_walk.description: Applies a user-supplied function to every member of an array.

array_key_exists.description: Returns true if the given key/index exists in the array. Unlike isset(), returns true even if value is null.

array_column.description: Returns the values from a single column of a multi-dimensional array.

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: A multi-dimensional array or array of objects

parameters.name: column_key
parameters.description: The column of values to return

parameters.name: index_key
parameters.description: The column to use as index/keys

array_chunk.description: Splits an array into chunks of the given size.

array_combine.description: Creates an array by using one array for keys and another for its values.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: Array to be used as keys

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Array to be used as values

array_diff.description: Computes the difference of arrays. Returns values in the first array that are not present in any of the other arrays.

array_intersect.description: Computes the intersection of arrays. Returns values present in the first array that also exist in all other arrays.

array_fill.description: Fills an array with the specified value, starting at the given index for a specified number of entries.

parameters.name: start_index
parameters.description: The first index of the returned array

compact.description: Creates an array containing variables and their values from the current symbol table.

parameters.name: var_name
parameters.description: Variable name or array of variable names

range.description: Creates an array containing a range of elements from start to end.

json_encode.description: Returns the JSON representation of a value.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: Maximum depth; must be greater than zero

json_decode.description: Decodes a JSON string. When associative is true, JSON objects are returned as associative arrays.

file_get_contents.signature: file_get_contents(string $filename, bool $use_include_path = false, ?resource $context = null, int $offset = 0, ?int $length = null): string|false
file_get_contents.description: Reads entire file into a string. Preferred way to read file contents. Supports HTTP/HTTPS URLs when allow_url_fopen is enabled.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Name of the file to read

file_put_contents.description: Writes data to a file. Equivalent to fopen(), fwrite(), and fclose() successively. Use FILE_APPEND to append.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Path to the file to write

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: The data to write (string, array, or stream resource)
